name: GPT-5.6 Sol
tagline: OpenAI's GPT-5.6 flagship preview, built for agentic coding, biology, and cybersecurity workflows.
seoTitle: GPT-5.6 Sol — OpenAI's June 2026 Flagship Preview
metaDescription: GPT-5.6 Sol is OpenAI's flagship model in the GPT-5.6 series, launched June 26, 2026 as a limited preview at $5/$30 per 1M tokens with new max and ultra modes.
overview: GPT-5.6 Sol is the flagship model in OpenAI's GPT-5.6 series, announced on June 26, 2026 alongside two smaller tiers, Terra and Luna. OpenAI describes Sol as its strongest model yet for complex reasoning, advanced coding, agent workflows, and demanding enterprise tasks.

Sol introduces a new max reasoning effort setting that gives the model more time to think, plus an ultra mode that delegates work to subagents to accelerate large, multi-step jobs. The model accepts text and image input and is positioned for agentic coding, scientific research, and high-stakes professional work.

Access at launch is restricted: in coordination with the U.S. government, OpenAI began with a limited preview to around 20 vetted partners through the API and Codex. General availability across ChatGPT, Codex, and the API is planned in the following weeks.
strengths: Frontier reasoning with a new max reasoning effort tier
Ultra mode that orchestrates subagents for long-horizon agentic work
State-of-the-art Terminal-Bench 2.1 score for command-line coding workflows
Stronger SecureBio biology and cybersecurity reasoning than GPT-5.5
Improved factuality, with fewer reported hallucinations than the prior flagship
useCases: Reach for it when an agentic coding task needs the most capable single model and the longest reasoning budget.
Reach for it for multi-agent workflows where Sol's ultra mode can spin up subagents to parallelise work.
Reach for it for high-stakes professional reasoning in scientific, biological, or cybersecurity domains.
Reach for it when factuality matters and the prior GPT-5.5 generation produced too many hallucinated facts.

comparisonTable.caption: GPT-5.6 Sol vs leading models on Terminal-Bench 2.1, as published by OpenAI at the GPT-5.6 preview launch.

pricing.note: Cached input reads receive a 90% discount; cache writes are billed at 1.25x the input rate, with a 30-minute minimum cache life.

links.label: GPT-5.6 Preview System Card (OpenAI Deployment Safety Hub)
OpenAI limits GPT-5.6 rollout after government request (TechCrunch)

faq.a: OpenAI announced GPT-5.6 Sol on June 26, 2026 as a limited preview, alongside the smaller Terra and Luna models in the GPT-5.6 series. Initial access was limited to roughly 20 vetted partner organisations via the API and Codex, with broader rollout planned in the following weeks.

faq.q: How much does GPT-5.6 Sol cost?
faq.a: GPT-5.6 Sol is priced at $5.00 per 1M input tokens and $30.00 per 1M output tokens, the same headline rate as GPT-5.5. Cached input reads receive a 90% discount, while cache writes are billed at 1.25x the input rate with a 30-minute minimum cache life.

faq.q: How does GPT-5.6 Sol compare to Claude Mythos 5 and GPT-5.5?
faq.a: On Terminal-Bench 2.1, OpenAI reports GPT-5.6 Sol at 88.8% (rising to 91.9% in ultra mode), versus 88.0% for Claude Mythos 5, 84.3% for both GPT-5.6 Terra and Claude Fable 5, and 83.4% for GPT-5.5. OpenAI also reports stronger SecureBio biology and cybersecurity reasoning than GPT-5.5, with reduced hallucination rates.

faq.q: What is ultra mode in GPT-5.6 Sol?
faq.a: Ultra mode is a new setting introduced with GPT-5.6 Sol that goes beyond single-agent reasoning by delegating subtasks to subagents to accelerate complex work. Combined with the new max reasoning effort, it lets Sol invest more compute on long-horizon, multi-step problems such as agentic coding.

versionHistory.note: Limited preview to vetted partners; broader rollout planned in following weeks.
